mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate reduces corporate financing costs, directly boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and rising recession probability, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) during high-volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across the broader economy and construction sector, reinforcing consumer spending cycles and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns in >55% of quarters or the interaction term shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production reflects immediate supply-side contraction and inventory drawdowns, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of declining INDPRO months or the signal reverses direction during monetary easing periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real corporate profit margins and increase inflation expectations, prompting immediate sell-offs in equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >65% of months with rising CPI or the correlation between CPI changes and 5-day returns falls below -0.05 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When real sales of new homes exceed 350,000 units, it confirms robust household balance sheets and strong consumer demand, leading to optimistic earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold signal yields negative returns in >50% of triggered months or loses predictive accuracy when housing policy interventions distort market fundamentals.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive quarterly returns in >55% of observations or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant during financial stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, sustained federal funds rates above 3.5% increase discount rates and compress equity valuations, directly causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns dominate in >60% of tightening months or the signal predictive power vanishes when growth data remains strong.
